_title: IEC 60364-5-52 / IEC 60529 — IP Code Requirements for Wiring Systems and Equipment
_reference: IEC 60364-5-52:2009+AMD1:2017, Clause 522 (External influences) and IEC 60529:2013 (Degrees of protection — IP code)
_note: IP codes define the degree of protection provided by an enclosure. The first digit = protection against solid objects (dust/contact); the second digit = protection against water ingress. IK codes (IEC 62262) describe protection against mechanical impact and are separate from IP. Always specify both dimensions when selecting equipment for exposed locations.

ip_code_explanation.format: IP [first digit] [second digit]  e.g. IP44, IP65, IPX8
ip_code_explanation.X_placeholder: An X in either digit position means that digit has not been rated — not that it is zero. IP2X is not the same as IP20.

additional_letters._use: Additional letters may be appended after the numeric code when supplementary information is needed, e.g. IP44C

first_digit_solid_objects._title: First IP Digit — Protection against solid objects and access

1.description: Protected against solid objects >50mm (e.g. back of hand — unintentional contact only, not deliberate intrusion)

2.test: 12.5mm sphere cannot enter; 80mm sphere cannot fully enter

4.description: Protected against solid objects >1mm (small tools, fine wires)

5.description: Dust protected — some dust ingress permitted but sufficient to prevent harmful deposit
5.test: Dust chamber test, negative pressure; no harmful deposit

6.description: Dust tight — no dust ingress whatsoever

second_digit_water_ingress._title: Second IP Digit — Protection against water ingress

1.description: Dripping water (vertical) — dripping water shall have no harmful effect

2.description: Dripping water when tilted up to 15° from vertical

3.description: Spraying water — water sprayed up to 60° from vertical shall have no harmful effect

4.description: Splashing water — water splashing from any direction shall have no harmful effect
4.test: Oscillating water spray, all directions, 10 min

5.description: Water jets — low-pressure water jets from any direction shall have no harmful effect

6.description: Powerful water jets — high-pressure water jets from any direction shall have no harmful effect

7.description: Temporary immersion — immersion to 1m depth for up to 30 minutes shall have no harmful effect
7.test: 0.15m above top of enclosure, 1m below water surface, 30 min

8.description: Continuous immersion — equipment suitable for continuous submersion under conditions specified by manufacturer (typically >1m)
8.test: Manufacturer-specified depth and time, agreed between manufacturer and user

9K.description: Powerful high-temperature water jets (additional K designation per IEC 60529 AMD 2:2013) — protection against close-range high pressure, high temperature spray

ik_mechanical_impact_codes._title: IK Code — Protection against mechanical impact (IEC 62262:2002)
ik_mechanical_impact_codes._note: IK is separate from IP. An enclosure can be IP65 but IK03 — excellent water/dust sealing but easily damaged by impact. Specify IK where vandalism or mechanical risk exists.

IK08.description: Heavy — industrial, public areas at risk of vandalism

IK09.description: Very heavy — heavy industrial, public infrastructure

minimum_ip_by_location._principle: Select the minimum IP for the external influence classification. IEC 60364-5-51 uses the AE, AF, AG classification system for external influences; IP is the direct translation for AE (water) and AB/AC (solid objects) ratings.

dry_indoor_domestic.description: Living rooms, offices, dry internal areas — domestic premises
dry_indoor_domestic.horizontal_top_surfaces: IP4X (to prevent objects falling in — clause 412.2.4)
dry_indoor_domestic.notes: IP2X is the minimum for shock protection (finger cannot touch live parts). Top surface of horizontal equipment must be IP4X to prevent object ingress.

damp_indoor.description: Kitchens (away from sink), utility rooms, laundry, unheated storage
damp_indoor.notes: Where condensation or cleaning water is present. Applies to all socket outlets and switches in areas regularly cleaned with water.

bathroom_zone_0.description: Inside bath or shower tray — permanently wet
bathroom_zone_0.notes: Temporary immersion protection required. Only FELV/SELV circuits ≤12V AC or ≤30V DC permitted. Fixed luminaires IP67 minimum. Reference: IEC 60364-7-701.

bathroom_zone_1.description: Directly above bath/shower to 2.25m height; shower cubicle walls
bathroom_zone_1.minimum_IP: IPX4 (IPX5 where jets used for cleaning)
bathroom_zone_1.notes: Socket outlets not permitted (except SELV shaver supply units). Switches prohibited unless SELV. Reference: IEC 60364-7-701.

bathroom_zone_2.description: 600mm horizontal distance beyond Zone 1, to 2.25m height; basin splash areas
bathroom_zone_2.notes: Socket outlets permitted only if SELV. Switches and junction boxes with appropriate IP. Reference: IEC 60364-7-701.

swimming_pool_zone_0.description: Inside the pool — permanently submerged during use
swimming_pool_zone_0.notes: Only fixed underwater luminaires and other fixed equipment permitted. FELV or SELV at ≤12V AC only. All equipment must be rated for continuous submersion.

swimming_pool_zone_1.description: Up to 2m above pool edge horizontally, up to 2.5m vertically
swimming_pool_zone_1.minimum_IP: IPX5 (IPX5 if jets/cleaning used; IPX7 if subject to flooding)
swimming_pool_zone_1.notes: Water jet cleaning IP5 typically applies. SELV circuits or fixed Class II equipment. Reference: IEC 60364-7-702.

swimming_pool_zone_2.description: 1.5m beyond Zone 1 boundary at pool edge
swimming_pool_zone_2.notes: Splash protection. General socket outlets permitted with RCD protection. Reference: IEC 60364-7-702.

outdoor_exposed.description: External areas — facades, car parks, external lighting columns, external sockets
outdoor_exposed.notes: IP44 is the minimum for outdoor use. IP54 or IP65 is strongly recommended for any equipment subject to direct rain, hosing, or pressure washing. Garden sockets and external switches: IP44 at minimum.

underground_buried.description: Junction boxes, terminations, and fittings in buried locations
underground_buried.notes: Continuous submersion IP8 required. Compound-filled or resin-sealed joints are preferred — verify manufacturer's IP68 test pressure and depth rating.

construction_site.description: Temporary installations on construction and demolition sites
construction_site.notes: Plug and socket outlets on construction sites: IP44 minimum per IEC 60364-7-704. Where high-pressure washing or flooding is foreseeable, IP54 or IP65. Socket outlets: IEC 60309 industrial pattern (BS EN 60309) for 16A and above.

agricultural_horticultural.description: Farm buildings, greenhouses, animal shelters, irrigation systems
agricultural_horticultural.notes: Corrosive atmospheres (ammonia from livestock): consider IP54 and stainless steel or GRP enclosures. Where cleaning with pressured water or steam: IP55. Reference: IEC 60364-7-705.

marina_waterside.description: Marina berths, boat pontoons, waterside pedestals
marina_waterside.notes: Salt-air environment requires additional corrosion protection beyond IP rating alone. Stainless steel fasteners, marine-grade enclosures. Socket outlets: IEC 60309 marine pattern. Reference: IEC 60364-7-708.

medical_group_2.description: Operating theatres, intensive care units — Group 2 medical locations
medical_group_2.notes: IP21 minimum (drip-proof) is common for general equipment. Higher IP where splashing or cleaning chemicals apply. Medical IT system enclosures: IP54 minimum for concealed locations. Reference: IEC 60364-7-710.

industrial_general.description: Factory floors, plant rooms, process areas
industrial_general.notes: Where metal swarf, oil mist, or cleaning chemicals present: IP54 or IP65. Where washing down with hose is routine: IP55 minimum.

common_under_specification_traps.trap: Using IP44 outdoor luminaires where pressure washing occurs
common_under_specification_traps.problem: IP44 tests with a fine spray; high-pressure washing forces water past seals. Use IP65 (dust-tight, jet-proof) or IP66 for pressure-wash environments.
common_under_specification_traps.fix: Specify IP65 minimum for all equipment in areas regularly pressure-washed.

common_under_specification_traps.trap: Assuming IPX4 in bathroom Zone 2 covers the whole bathroom
common_under_specification_traps.problem: Zone 0 and 1 are stricter. A luminaire rated IPX4 above a bath (Zone 1) fails the requirement — it needs IPX4 minimum but also must be rated for Zone 1 conditions.
common_under_specification_traps.fix: Check zone boundaries carefully. Zone 0 is inside the tub/shower floor. Zone 1 is directly above. Check every fitting's installed position.

common_under_specification_traps.trap: IP rating without IK rating in public or vandal-risk areas
common_under_specification_traps.problem: IP68 enclosure with IK04 can be smashed open. IP and IK are independent.
common_under_specification_traps.fix: Specify IK08 or IK10 for external luminaires, surface boxes, and distribution boards in public areas.

common_under_specification_traps.trap: Mounting an IP22 distribution board in a damp plant room
common_under_specification_traps.problem: IP22 protects against drip when tilted 15°. Condensation, hose-down, or pressured water cleaning penetrates IP22 easily.
common_under_specification_traps.fix: IP44 minimum for all enclosures in damp, plant room, or outdoor locations.

common_under_specification_traps.trap: Using domestic IP2X switches and sockets in commercial kitchens
common_under_specification_traps.problem: Commercial kitchens are routinely hosed down. IP2X provides only finger protection — no water resistance.
common_under_specification_traps.fix: IP65 switches and sockets in commercial kitchen locations.
